title: Stay the Course
content: You know, I've seen a lot of market crashes in my time. The crash of '87, the dot-com bubble, 2008... Each time, people panic. They sell at the worst possible moment. I remember in 2008, investors were calling, terrified. 'Jack, should I sell everything?' And I'd tell them what I always tell people: Stay the course. The market has always recovered. Always. It might take time, but it comes back. The worst thing you can do is sell when everyone else is selling. That's when prices are lowest. That's when you should be buying, if anything. I know it's hard. Fear is powerful. But discipline... discipline is more powerful. The investors who stayed the course in 2008? They recovered everything and more. The ones who sold in panic? Many never recovered. Stay the course. It's not just advice. It's a philosophy for investing and for life.
